assumptions.bytesNote: All events 1200 B/event (locked single constant, 2026-06-03). The PROCTITLE exclude removes ~100 B from each execve event; the PATH exclude removes ~200 B from each file-touch event. The cost band below reflects the ADDITIVE rules priced at the full 1200 B/event (upper bound). True net is <= this band. Standardized from mixed (900 watch/1200 syscall) to single 1200 B/event.

correct_model.comment: Cost band = additive rules only. Both exclude rules contribute 0 additive volume and REDUCE net bytes. Net cost <= this band.
correct_model.math: execve (MEDIUM, 1200 B): typical 15000*1200/1e9 = 0.018 GB/day. Watches (1200 B): typical 200*1200/1e9 = 0.00024 GB/day. Total typical: 0.01824 GB/day. Cost: 0.01824*30.4*5 = $2.77/mo. True net is <= $2.77/mo. Standardized from mixed (900 watch/1200 syscall) to single 1200 B/event (locked 2026-06-03).
correct_model.note: Net <= band. The PROCTITLE exclude removes ~100 B per execve event; the PATH exclude removes ~200 B per watch event. The exact suppression benefit is workload-dependent (f3 section 5).

naive_wrong_model.comment: A naive impl treats 'always' action in the exclude rules as additive (same as 'always,exit'). Counts each exclude rule as its own MEDIUM-tier syscall rule.
naive_wrong_model.error: ~3x overstatement of typical cost. The two exclude rules contribute 0 additive volume; they only suppress. Counting them as additive MEDIUM (15000 events * 1200 B each) adds two spurious MEDIUM bands, inflating cost from $2.77/mo to ~$8.21/mo. Direction (suppressive, not additive) is the load-bearing assertion.
